name: Iconography Lab
description: Define the instantly recognizable visual and verbal codes of a public figure, including palette, props, silhouettes, poses, moods, and catchphrases, then output an identity kit.
short_description: Map visual and verbal codes into an identity kit.
audience: Creative directors shaping personality-led visual systems
Brand strategists finding durable cues in public references
Talent and founder teams turning recognizable codes into practical guidance
detail_lines: Clusters photo sets, captions, slogans, props, wardrobe, gestures, and moods into reusable visual and verbal code systems.
Exports palette, prop, silhouette, framing, phrase, and do-or-do-not guidance for future content, events, and merch.
Works only from public or user-authorized material and flags impersonation, likeness misuse, and overly close copying risks.
